rationale: Codebase-pin lens: every present-tense pin resolves in this tree. SkillFiles()/go:embed (embed.go:19-45), installSkills (setup/skills.go:105) called by RunClaude (claude.go:159), PanelGateDecision (panel/gate.go:131), attributeDomain (ownership.go:361) + the spec-108 TestWorkflowOwnsTraceAndGolangci precedent (ownership_wave2_test.go:37) all exist and match the accessors/tests the spec proposes to mirror. workflow OWNERSHIP.yaml claims .claude/skills/**, plugins/mindspec/**, internal/{validate,setup,panel}/** and the OWNERSHIP file itself but NOT .claude/workflows/** — so R9's added claim and its 'all other edited paths already covered' claim are both exactly right. isDocFile (docsync.go:393) and isProcessArtifact (divergence.go:300) both return false for .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js, confirming R9's governable-source + same-diff-or-earlier reasoning. All R7-named skill sections exist in both skills. Every 109/110-dependent pin is honestly attributed as prerequisite-not-present: no panel subcommand wired (110 verbs absent), no runner:/claude_sub_on_quota/panel: mix in internal/config (109 absent), ADR-0040 has no file (prose-only, correctly kept out of touchpoints). The four cited ADR touchpoints (0034/0035/0036/0037) all resolve, satisfying 110 R6. Zero false pins.

findings.summary: R7 calls ms-panel-tally's 'Consolidate' a section, but it is Step 4 inside `## Steps`, not a top-level header. The AC greps the bare string 'Consolidate' (satisfiable). Cosmetic imprecision, not a false pin.

Current ms-panel-run substitution contract writes reviewer_id "R<slot> claude-sub" (SKILL.md:183); R4 abbreviates to "<slot> claude-sub". Equivalent when slot id is e.g. R4; the plan should keep the R-prefixed form for verdict comparability.

Current embed glob is skills/*/SKILL.md (nested per-skill dirs); R8 proposes //go:embed workflows/* over flat .js files, so WorkflowFiles() walks flat files rather than <name>/SKILL.md. Directionally a valid mirror; the exact walk shape is a plan-time detail, not a spec-approval blocker.
